classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

The Canons of the Council in Trullo.

metadata.author: The Canons of the Council in Trullo; Often Called The Quinisext Council

title: Canon VI.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: Since it is declared in the apostolic canons that of those who are advanced to the clergy unmarried, only lectors and cantors are able to marry; we also, maintaining this, determine that henceforth it is in nowise lawful for any subdeacon, deacon or presbyter after his ordination to contract matrimony but if he shall have dared to do so, let him be deposed.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: And if any of those who enter the clergy, wishes to be joined to a wife in lawful marriage before he is ordained subdeacon, deacon, or presbyter, let it be done.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: If any ordained person contracts matrimony, let him be deposed.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: If he wishes to be married he should become so before his ordination.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: Aristenus points out how this canon annuls the tenth canon of Ancyra, which allows a deacon and even a presbyter to marry after ordination and continue in his ministry, provided at the time of his ordination he had in the presence of witnesses declared his inability to remain chaste or his desire to marry.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: This present canon follows the XXVIth of the Apostolic canons.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
verses.text: The last clause of this canon, limited in its application to subdeacons, is found in the Corpus Juris Canonici, Gratian’s Decretum, Pars I., Dist.

verses.book: The Canons of the Council in Trullo.
